mapper.sql: select r.rule_id, r.scene_id, r.fee_id, s.scene_code, s.scene_name, s.business_domain, f.fee_code, f.fee_name, r.rule_code, r.rule_name, r.rule_type, r.condition_logic, r.priority, r.quantity_variable_code, qv.variable_name as quantity_variable_name, r.pricing_mode, r.pricing_json, r.amount_formula, r.note_template, r.status, r.sort_no, r.remark, r.create_by, r.create_time, r.update_by, r.update_time, (select count(1) from cost_rule_condition c where c.rule_id = r.rule_id) as condition_count, (select count(1) from cost_rule_tier t where t.rule_id = r.rule_id) as tier_count, (select group_concat(concat(c.display_name, ' ', c.operator_code, ' ', ifnull(c.compare_value, '')) order by c.group_no asc, c.sort_no asc separator '; ') from cost_rule_condition c where c.rule_id = r.rule_id and c.status = '0') as condition_summary from cost_rule r left join cost_scene s on s.scene_id = r.scene_id left join cost_fee_item f on f.fee_id = r.fee_id left join cost_variable qv on qv.scene_id = r.scene_id and qv.variable_code = r.quantity_variable_code

where.if: and ( r.rule_code like concat('%', #{keyword}, '%') or r.rule_name like concat('%', #{keyword}, '%') or f.fee_code like concat('%', #{keyword}, '%') or f.fee_name like concat('%', #{keyword}, '%') )

mapper.select: order by r.priority desc, r.sort_no asc, r.rule_id desc

select count(1) as ruleCount, coalesce(sum(case when r.status = '0' then 1 else 0 end), 0) as enabledRuleCount, coalesce(sum(case when r.rule_type = 'TIER_RATE' then 1 else 0 end), 0) as tierRuleCount, coalesce(sum(case when r.rule_type = 'FORMULA' then 1 else 0 end), 0) as formulaRuleCount from cost_rule r left join cost_scene s on s.scene_id = r.scene_id left join cost_fee_item f on f.fee_id = r.fee_id

select r.rule_id as ruleId, r.scene_id as sceneId, r.fee_id as feeId, s.scene_code as sceneCode, s.scene_name as sceneName, f.fee_code as feeCode, f.fee_name as feeName, r.rule_code as ruleCode, r.rule_name as ruleName, r.status as status, (select count(1) from cost_rule_condition c where c.rule_id = r.rule_id) as conditionCount, (select count(1) from cost_rule_tier t where t.rule_id = r.rule_id) as tierCount, (select count(1) from cost_publish_snapshot snap join cost_publish_version ver on ver.version_id = snap.version_id where ver.scene_id = r.scene_id and snap.snapshot_type = 'RULE' and snap.object_code = r.rule_code) as publishedVersionCount, (select count(1) from cost_result_trace td where td.rule_id = r.rule_id) as traceCount from cost_rule r left join cost_scene s on s.scene_id = r.scene_id left join cost_fee_item f on f.fee_id = r.fee_id where r.rule_id = #{ruleId}

select c.condition_id, c.scene_id, c.rule_id, c.group_no, c.sort_no, c.variable_code, c.display_name, c.operator_code, c.compare_value, c.status, c.remark, v.variable_name, v.variable_type, v.data_type, v.dict_type from cost_rule_condition c left join cost_variable v on v.scene_id = c.scene_id and v.variable_code = c.variable_code where c.rule_id = #{ruleId} order by c.group_no asc, c.sort_no asc, c.condition_id asc

select t.tier_id, t.scene_id, t.rule_id, t.start_value, t.end_value, t.rate_value, t.interval_mode, t.tier_no, t.status, t.remark, concat( ifnull(cast(t.start_value as char), '-INF'), case when t.interval_mode = 'LEFT_OPEN_RIGHT_CLOSED' then ' < x <= ' else ' <= x < ' end, ifnull(cast(t.end_value as char), '+INF') ) as range_summary from cost_rule_tier t where t.rule_id = #{ruleId} order by t.tier_no asc, t.tier_id asc
